the >] shape strips surrounding single quotes (quote="'", content-exclusive label)

the mixed dash+pipe form `--|label|-->` is non-standard; it must WARN (no block / no nodes+edges), NOT silently succeed with the pipe chars leaked into the label value
thick analogue `==|label|==>` of the mixed dash+pipe form must WARN, not leak the pipe chars into the label as a false-green
control: the canonical pipe-label form `-->|label|` strips the pipes → label='label'
control: the canonical inline-dash form `-- label -->` yields label='label'
a longer closing shaft `--->` is one arrow; the extra shaft chars must NOT leak into the label (label='text', not 'text -')
closing shaft `---->` consumed whole; label stays 'text' (was leaking 'text --')
any shaft length closes cleanly; label stays 'text' (was leaking 'text ---')
thick: longer closing shaft `===>` is one arrow; label stays 'text' (was leaking 'text =')
thick: closing shaft `====>` consumed whole; label stays 'text' (was leaking 'text ==')

internal hyphens inside the label are preserved — only a shaft run that reaches `>` is a terminator

control: the canonical pipe-label form `-->|label|` strips the pipes → label='label'
an asymmetric label may not begin with an unquoted opening bracket; `A>(text)]` must WARN/refuse, not corrupt to label '(text)'

a bare hyphenated id is read as the full id receive-order; the node span covers the full text so slice(startChar,endChar)===id
a bare (unlabeled) thick arrow with a 3-char shaft parses to one edge A->B, like the bare dash long-shaft form; the extra `=` is a length hint, not a parse error
a bare thick arrow with a 4-char shaft parses to one edge A->B; any shaft length closes cleanly
the compact (no-space) bare long thick arrow also parses to one edge A->B
a real block mixing a normal arrow with a long thick arrow keeps BOTH edges; neither line fails the whole block
